error_signature: loading large files into memory causes crashes
title: Memory issues when uploading large video files to YouTube
symptom: When attempting to upload large video files (1GB+) to YouTube using n8n, the workflow crashes or experiences performance issues due to memory limitations.
not_this_if: Using external platforms for video uploads
root_cause: n8n loads the entire file into memory when using the 'Read File from Disk' node, which can exceed the available memory on the server, especially with limited RAM. This leads to crashes or degraded performance during the upload process.
fix_steps: Set the environment variable N8N_DEFAULT_BINARY_DATA_MODE=filesystem to store large binary files on disk instead of in memory.
Restart the n8n instance to apply the changes.
Use the YouTube node to upload the video files without exceeding memory limits.
agent_instruction: Set the N8N_DEFAULT_BINARY_DATA_MODE to filesystem and restart n8n.
